title: modbus-adapter southbound adapter configuration
description: The configuration the Modbus adapter itself understands: the object at `component.global`, with each entry of `component.instances[]` validated against `#/$defs/device`. A southbound adapter models ONE DEVICE PER INSTANCE — how to reach it, how often to poll it, what to decode, and what it is permitted to write. See docs/SOUTHBOUND.md §2.1.1 for the Modbus mapping. The library envelope (logging, messaging, metricEmission, heartbeat, hierarchy, identity, topic, tags, and `component.token`) is owned by the canonical schema (schema/edgecommons-config-schema.json) and is deliberately NOT redeclared here.

staleSignalSecs.description: A signal with no successful read for longer than this counts toward `southbound_health.staleSignals`. A signal that silently stops updating is otherwise indistinguishable from one that is simply not changing — which is why staleness is measured rather than assumed.

defaults.description: Timing/publish defaults resolved signal/group ▸ instance ▸ global ▸ built-in.

pollIntervalMs.description: Milliseconds between reads of a poll group.

publishMode.description: `onChange` publishes when a value changes past its deadband; `always` publishes every poll. Any other value is treated as `onChange`.

batchMs.description: If > 0, buffer a signal's samples and publish one message per `batchMs`; 0 publishes each immediately.

maxGap.description: Max address gap the poller bridges when coalescing signals into one Modbus read.

device.title: One Modbus device (instance)
device.description: One entry of `component.instances[]`: a single Modbus slave / gateway. Each device runs on its own worker thread and publishes to its own UNS data topics (ecv1/{device}/modbus-adapter/{instance}/data/{signal}).

id.description: Unique device id. It is the `{instance}` token of this device's UNS topics and the `instance` dimension of its metrics, so it must be a valid UNS token (lower-kebab) and stable.

adapter.description: The protocol backend, published in `device.adapter` of every SouthboundSignalUpdate. Always `modbus` for this adapter.

batchMs.description: Buffer window; 0 publishes each sample immediately.

allow.description: Signal ids this device may write, matched on the STABLE `signal.id` (e.g. `u1/holding/40/uint16`), checked before any device I/O. Anything not listed is refused, whatever an `sb/write` command asks for. An empty list — the default — makes the device read-only, the correct posture for anything touching a control system.

pollGroups.description: Groups of signals polled together on one interval against one unit id.

connection.description: How to reach the Modbus slave: TCP, serial RTU, or RTU-over-TCP. Published in `device.endpoint`.

unitId.description: Default Modbus unit/slave id (overridable per poll group / signal-ref).

pollGroup.description: Signals read together on one interval against one unit id.

id.description: Stable group id (logs, metrics, `sb/signals`). Defaults to `group-N`.

pollIntervalMs.description: How often this group is read. Falls back to the instance/global default.

unitId.description: Modbus unit id for this group's reads. Falls back to `connection.unitId`.

publishMode.description: `onChange` / `always`. Falls back to the instance/global default.

maxGap.description: Coalescing gap (registers/bits). Falls back to the instance/global default.

signal.description: One Modbus signal: a named, typed view onto a register/bit address.

name.description: Human name; the sanitized `data` channel token and the friendly read/write ref.

table.description: The Modbus table. `coil`/`holding` are writable; `discrete`/`input` are read-only.

type.description: Decoded value type. Bit tables (`coil`/`discrete`) are always `bool`; register tables default to `uint16`.

bit.description: Extract one bit of a holding/input register as a bool.

scale.description: Linear scale applied to numeric values.

offset.description: Linear offset applied to numeric values.
